description:
Kalispell fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/topics/kalispell/Schools.tsx: The library system name is wrong. The page says "Imaginary Nest Public Library," but the Flathead Valley public library system is ImagineIF Libraries.

2. web/components/town/topics/kalispell/CostOfLiving.tsx: The population sentence conflates the city and the county. It says Kalispell has "a population of roughly 29,886 in Flathead County," which reads as though 29,886 is the county population. That figure is being used for Kalispell itself, while Flathead County is much larger.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Kalispell fishing metadata is internally inconsistent. The fishing meta description says 11 access sites, while the guide description and the outdoor-recreation FAQ use 10 public access sites within 30 miles.

Evidence checked during review: internal Kalispell page comparisons, Grokipedia-backed Kalispell searches, and the named Flathead Valley institutions referenced in the current copy.
title: Fact-check Kalispell pages

description:
Billings fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Billings school metadata appears stale. The meta description says Billings Public Schools has 16,000 students, but Grokipedia's Billings Public Schools coverage reports 16,570 students across the district.

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Billings fishing metadata is stale relative to the current 30-mile content. The fishing meta description and guide description still say 20 fishing access sites, while the hub intro, FAQ, and fishing page body use 8 fishing access sites within 30 miles.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and related Billings cost-of-living copy: Billings is described as having the most affordable housing of any major Montana city, but the site also gives Great Falls a lower affordability ratio (5.1 vs Billings at 5.4). That superlative should be corrected or removed.

Evidence checked during review: Grokipedia's Billings Public Schools coverage, internal Billings page comparisons, and cross-checks against other site affordability figures.
title: Fact-check Billings pages

description:
Missoula fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/topics/Schools.tsx: MCPS structure is wrong. The page says the district has only two middle schools (Washington and Meadow Hill) and frames Big Sky High as being in the surrounding area. Official MCPS sources show C.S. Porter Middle School is also an MCPS middle school, and Big Sky High School is an MCPS grades 9-12 high school in Missoula.

2. web/components/town/topics/Schools.tsx: The Missoula Public Library opening year is wrong. The page says the new building opened in 2022, but the new library opened and celebrated its grand opening in 2021.

3. web/components/town/topics/Hiking.tsx: The Bob Marshall Wilderness proximity claim is overstated. Saying the complex is reachable within about an hour's drive from Missoula appears too aggressive; official Lolo National Forest access information places the Seeley Lake access area about 60 miles northeast of Missoula, with wilderness trailheads farther beyond that.

4. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Missoula hiking metadata is stale. The Missoula hiking meta description says 90 trailheads near Missoula, while the page body and current 30-mile content use 49 trailheads within 30 miles.

5. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Missoula fishing metadata is stale. The Missoula fishing meta description says 44 fishing access sites, while the page body and FAQ use 28 fishing access sites within 30 miles.

Evidence checked during review: official MCPS school pages for Big Sky High and C.S. Porter Middle School, official UM enrollment pages, official Destination Missoula fishing page, authoritative Missoula Public Library opening coverage, and Lolo National Forest access information.
title: Fact-check Missoula pages

description:

description:
Bozeman fact-check findings to fix:

1. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: Bozeman's Yellowstone north-entrance distance is wrong in the hub intro and FAQ. The current copy says Yellowstone's north entrance at Gardiner is 48 miles from Bozeman, but another Bozeman page already says the north entrance is roughly 80 miles south, and external route references place the drive at about 78 to 80 miles.

2. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts and web/components/town/topics/bozeman/WeekendItinerary.tsx: Bridger Bowl distance is inconsistent and too short in some Bozeman copy. Grokipedia describes Bridger Bowl as approximately 16 miles northeast of Bozeman, but the hub FAQ and weekend itinerary use 12 miles.

3. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts: The outdoor-recreation FAQ contains an internal contradiction. It says Bozeman has 1 ski area within 30 miles, then names both Bridger Bowl and Big Sky in the same parenthetical. That should be reconciled, especially because other Bozeman copy places Big Sky much farther away.

4. web/components/town/cluster-data.ts versus web/components/town/topics/bozeman/Jobs.tsx and web/components/town/topics/bozeman/WeekendItinerary.tsx: Big Sky distance is internally inconsistent across Bozeman pages. Some copy says 32 miles from Bozeman, while the jobs page says 45 miles southwest. The site should use one verified distance/routing convention.

Evidence checked during review: Grokipedia's Bridger Bowl page, Grokipedia-backed Bozeman recreation searches, internal Bozeman page comparisons, and route references for Bozeman to Gardiner.
title: Fact-check Bozeman pages
